mnemonics.title: Types of Firewalls
mnemonics.content: P.S.P.N. - 'Packet Stateful Proxy NGFW' - Remember: 'PackeTS Serve Protection Networks'. The four main firewall types: Packet Filtering, Stateful Inspection, Proxy, and Next-Generation Firewall.

mnemonics.title: Firewall Characteristics
mnemonics.content: F.L.N.V.I.B. - 'Filtering Logging NAT VPN Inspection Blocking' - Remember: 'Fast Lions Never Visit Indian Bars'. These are the key characteristics/features of firewalls.

mnemonics.title: Firewall Layers
mnemonics.content: P.S.A. - 'Packet Stateful Application' - Remember: 'People Stay Alert'. Represents the three main layers where firewalls operate: Network layer (Packet), Connection state (Stateful), Application layer (Proxy).

acronyms.usage: A isolated network segment between internal and external networks where public-facing servers are placed. Firewalls protect this zone.
Advanced firewall combining traditional filtering with deep packet inspection, application awareness, and intrusion prevention. Use when discussing modern firewall capabilities.
List of rules defining which traffic is allowed or denied. Used by packet filtering firewalls to make filtering decisions.
Maps internal private IP addresses to public IPs. Firewalls often perform NAT to hide internal network topology.

acronyms.expansion: Intrusion Detection System / Intrusion Prevention System
acronyms.usage: IDS monitors and alerts; IPS actively blocks threats. Often integrated into NGFWs for comprehensive protection.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a hotel lobby where security checks each person's ID card (packet header) but doesn't care about what they're carrying inside. Fast but basic - only checks source/destination IP, ports, and protocols.

Imagine a border security checkpoint that not only checks your ID but also tracks your entire journey - where you came from, your visa status, and maintains a log of your entry. It remembers the context of connections.

Think of a highly protective receptionist who intercepts every call, checks if the request is safe, and then makes the call on your behalf. The outside world never directly contacts you - the proxy intermediates.

Imagine a futuristic security checkpoint with multiple layers: X-ray machines (deep packet inspection), face recognition (application awareness), threat intelligence database (IPS), and smart gates (advanced filtering) - all working together.
Visualize your network as a castle with a moat (firewall) surrounding it. The drawbridge (ports) can be raised or lowered based on who requests entry, with guards (rules) checking every visitor.

analogies.analogy: Like a bouncer at a club checking your ID at the door - only looks at basic credentials (name, age) but doesn't care what's in your pockets. Fast but limited security.

Like a travel agent who books flights on your behalf. You give your requirements to the agent, and the agent interacts with the airline directly - the airline never knows your real identity or details.
Like a telephone operator who not only connects your call but monitors the entire conversation to ensure it stays legitimate, tracking both ends of the communication.
Like a reception area in a building - it's between the outside world (street) and the secure internal offices. Visitors wait here, and staff bring items to them, protecting the inner offices.

Like a workplace attendance policy: 'Allow employees (port 80/443 traffic) through main gate during working hours (9-5), but block unauthorized visitors (unknown ports) and delivery persons (certain protocols) except scheduled deliveries.'
